volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Stichus (Section 4.1.549 - Section 4.2.612)

sections.label: Section 4.1.549
sections.original: Quis istuc dicit ? an ille quasi tu ?
sections.translation: Who says that ? Does he say it [*] just as though you were to say it ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.549b
sections.translation: Just as I now say it to you .

sections.label: Section 4.1.550
sections.original: Immo duas dabo , inquit ille adulescens “ una si parumst ;
sections.translation: Aye , I ’ ll give you two of them , says this young man , if one ’ s too little ; and if you are not satisfied with two , says he , two more shall be added .

sections.label: Section 4.1.552
sections.original: Quis istuc quaeso ? an ille quasi ego ?
sections.translation: Prithee , who says that ? Does he say it just as though I were to say it ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.552b
sections.original: Is ipse quasi tu . tum senex
sections.translation: He says it just as though you were to say it . Then says this old gentleman , just as though I were to say it , Well , give me four , if you like , so long only as , i ’ faith , you find them something to eat as well , that they mayn ’ t consume my victuals .

sections.label: Section 4.1.555
sections.original: Videlicet parcum illum fuisse senem , qui illi istaec dixerit ,
sections.translation: Why surely it must have been a stingy old chap to say that , in asking food as well of him who promised them to him .

sections.label: Section 4.1.557
sections.original: Videlicet non fuisse illum aequom ádulescentem , qui ilico
sections.translation: Why surely , this young man must have been a good-for-nothing fellow , who forthwith , when the other asked him , refused to give him a grain of wheat . But , i ’ troth , the old gentleman asked what was fair , inasmuch as

sections.label: Section 4.1.560
sections.original: filiae illae dederat dotem , accipere pro tibicina .
sections.translation: the dowry which he had given to his daughter , he wished him to have as an equivalent for the music-girl .

sections.label: Section 4.1.561
sections.original: Hercle ille quidem certo adulescens docte vorsutus fuit ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , for sure I really do think that the young man was well advised , who wouldn ’ t give a mistress to that old fellow in return for the dower .

sections.label: Section 4.1.563
sections.original: Senex quidem voluit , si posset , indipisci de cibo ;
sections.translation: The old gentleman wished , indeed , if he could , to bargain for their maintenance ; because he couldn ’ t , he said he wished it to be done on what terms it might .

sections.label: Section 4.1.565
sections.original: fiat ille inquít adulescens . facis benigne inquit senex .
sections.translation: Done , said this young man . You do me a kindness , said the old gentleman . Have I the thing agreed upon ? said he . I ’ ll do even as you wish it to be done , said the other . But I ’ ll be off in-doors , and congratulate my daughters on your arrival . Then I ’ ll go wash me at the bath [*] ; there will I take all care of my old age ; after that , when I ’ ve bathed , lying down , I ’ ll await you at my leisure .

sections.label: Section 4.1.570
sections.original: Graphicum mortalem Antiphonem , ut apologum fecit quam fabre .
sections.translation: A funny mortal , Antipho ; how cleverly he did make up his story .

sections.label: Section 4.1.571
sections.original: Etiam nunc scelestus se esse ducit pro adulescentulo .
sections.translation: Even yet the rogue considers himself a young man . A mistress shall be given to the fellow , to sing to the old chap at night in bed ; for , i ’ faith , indeed , I know not of what other use a mistress can be to him .

sections.label: Section 4.1.574
sections.original: Sed quid agit parasitus noster Gelasimus ? etiam valet ?
sections.translation: But how fares our Parasite , Gelasimus ? Is he well , too ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.575
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I saw the fellow not so very long ago .

sections.label: Section 4.1.575b
sections.translation: How fares he ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.575c
sections.translation: Like one half-starved .

sections.label: Section 4.1.576
sections.translation: Why didn ’ t you invite the fellow to your house to dinner ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.576b
sections.translation: That on my arrival I mightn ’ t be wasting anything . But see , here ’ s the wolf in the Fable [*] ; here he is in person with his ravenous fit .

sections.label: Section 4.1.578
sections.translation: We ’ ll have some sport with the fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.1.578b
sections.translation: You put me in mind of a plan I had already resolved on .

sections.label: Section 4.2.579
sections.original: Sed ita ut occepi narrare vobis : quom hic non adfui ,
sections.translation: But as I had begun to tell you ; while I have been absent hence ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.580
sections.original: cúm amicis deliberavi iam et cum cognatis meis .
sections.translation: I ’ ve now been consulting with my friends and with my relatives . They have been my advisers to the effect that I should this very day kill myself with starvation . But don ’ t I see Pamphilus with his brother Epignomus ? Yes , ’ tis he . I ’ ll accost the man . ( Goes up to PAMPHILUS .) O longed-for Pamphilus ! O my salvation ! O my life ! O my delight ! right welcome . I rejoice that

sections.label: Section 4.2.585
sections.translation: you ’ ve returned safe from abroad to your native land . Welcome .

sections.label: Section 4.2.585b
sections.translation: Welcome , Gelasimus .

sections.label: Section 4.2.586
sections.translation: Have you been quite well ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.586b
sections.translation: I have taken good care of my health .

sections.label: Section 4.2.586c
sections.translation: I ’ troth , I ’ m glad of it . I ’ faith , I confoundedly wish I had now a thousand measures of silver .

sections.label: Section 4.2.588
sections.translation: What need have you of it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.588b
sections.original: Hunc hercle ad cenam ut vocem , te non vocem .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , that I might invite him to dinner , and not invite you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.589
sections.translation: You are talking against your own interest .

sections.label: Section 4.2.589b
sections.translation: This , then , that I might invite you both

sections.label: Section 4.2.590
sections.original: et equidem simitu hau maligne vos invitassem domum
sections.translation: ⋯ for my part ⋯ I should not avoid [*] ⋯ there is nothing so ⋯ as this ⋯

sections.label: Section 4.2.592
sections.original: Edepol te vocem lubenter , si superfiat locus .
sections.translation: Troth , now , I ’ d ask you with pleasure , if there were room left .

sections.label: Section 4.2.593
sections.original: Quin tum stans obstrusero aliquid strenue .
sections.translation: Well , standing , then , I ’ ll gobble down a bit in the scramble .

sections.label: Section 4.2.593b
sections.translation: No , only this one thing can be done .

sections.label: Section 4.2.594
sections.translation: What ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.594b
sections.translation: When the guests have gone , that then you may come —

sections.label: Section 4.2.594c
sections.translation: Hurra ! capital !

sections.label: Section 4.2.595
sections.original: Vasa lautum , non ad cenam dico .
sections.translation: To wash the pots , I mean ; not to dinner .

sections.label: Section 4.2.595b
sections.translation: The Gods confound you ! What say you , Pamphilus ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.596b
sections.original: Ad cenam hercle alio promisi foras .
sections.translation: I ’ troth , this day I ’ m engaged to dine elsewhere abroad .

sections.label: Section 4.2.597
sections.translation: How , abroad ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.597b
sections.translation: Really abroad , on my word .

sections.label: Section 4.2.597c
sections.translation: How the plague do you like , thus wearied , to be supping abroad ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.598b
sections.translation: Which do you advise me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.598c
sections.translation: Order a dinner to be cooked at home , and word to be sent to him who invited you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.599b
sections.translation: Shall I dine at home , alone ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.600
sections.translation: Why , not alone ; invite me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.600b
sections.translation: But I ’ m afraid lest he should scold me , who has been to this expense for my sake .

sections.label: Section 4.2.601b
sections.translation: It may easily be excused — only listen to me ; do order a dinner to be cooked at home .

sections.label: Section 4.2.602b
sections.translation: Not by my advice , indeed , will he act so as to disappoint that person this day .

sections.label: Section 4.2.603b
sections.translation: Will you not be off from here ? Perhaps you suppose that I don ’ t see what you ’ re about . Do you look to yourself , please .

sections.label: Section 4.2.605
sections.original: nam illic homó tuam hereditatem inhiát , quasi esuriens lupus .
sections.translation: ( To PAMPHILUS .) How that fellow is gaping after your property just like a hungry wolf . Don ’ t you know how men are set upon here in the street at night ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.607
sections.original: Tanto pluris qui defendant ire advorsum iussero .
sections.translation: So many the more servants will I bid to come and fetch me , that they may protect me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.608
sections.original: Non it , non it , quia tanto opere suades ne ebitat .
sections.translation: He won ’ t stir — he won ’ t stir ; because you persuade him so earnestly not to go out .

sections.label: Section 4.2.608b
sections.translation: Do order a dinner to be cooked at home with all speed for me and for yourself and your wife .

sections.label: Section 4.2.610
sections.original: si hercle faxis , non opinor dices deceptum fore .
sections.translation: Troth , if you do so , I don ’ t think you ’ ll say that you are deceived .

sections.label: Section 4.2.611
sections.original: Per hanc tibi cenam incenato , Gelasime , esse hodie licet .
sections.translation: So far as that dinner is concerned , Gelasimus , you may be dinnerless to-day .

sections.label: Section 4.2.612
sections.translation: Are you going abroad to dine ?
